mnemonics: Davie's D's: Differentiated Services using Dynamic Packet State
keyPoints: It is a stateless core network architecture that pushes complexity to the edge.
It uses the DSCP (Differentiated Services Code Point) field in the IP header to mark packets for a specific Per-Hop Behavior (PHB).
Routers in the core simply forward packets based on their marked class, making it highly scalable.
commonMistakes: Confusing DiffServ (stateless, scalable) with IntServ (stateful, resource-intensive).
Assuming DiffServ provides absolute bandwidth guarantees; it only provides relative prioritization and classes of service.
